title: API.md missing entries for v1 APIs added since reorient
description: API.md predates several APIs that have since landed and is now incomplete. Missing: reg-route, match-url, route-url, route-link (EP 012); reg-machine-guard, reg-machine-action (used in login example, mentioned in Construction Prompts CP-5 and 005-StateMachines); render-to-string, :rf/hydrate event, :rf/server-init event, :nav/push-url fx, :platforms metadata key on reg-fx (EP 011); reg-event-error-handler (009 §Error contract); compute-sub (008 + tests use it); bound-fn / bound-dispatcher / dispatcher / subscriber (002/004 affordances). Sweep API.md and bring it back in sync with the per-EP docs.

title: API.md missing from README index and reading order
description: API.md is not listed in README.md's documents table or in the reading order. Should appear in both — it's the consolidated API reference used by Construction Prompts and (in spirit) by anyone implementing the pattern.

title: Broken anchor: 004:273 → 002§what-frame-provider-is
description: 004-Views.md line 273 links to (002-Frames.md#what-frame-provider-is) but the section was renamed to 'What `frame-provider` is (CLJS reference)' which generates anchor what-frame-provider-is-cljs-reference. Update the link in 004 to the new anchor.

title: Reconcile EP 006 substrate status after the SSR reorientation
description: Possible investigation: after SSR became a first-class goal, 000 now frames substrate decoupling as being pulled toward v1 relevance, while some index/status text may still describe EP 006 as cost-benefit-gated and post-v1. Verify whether this is a real staging change or only a pressure signal, then align the wording accordingly. This is a staging review point, not a decision.
close_reason: EP 006 status reconciled. 000-Vision OQ-7 disposition updated: substrate decoupling no longer feasibility-gated; EP 006 is v1 territory because EP 011 (SSR) requires substrate-agnostic rendering. README index entry for EP 006 updated to match. Cooperative-rendering-substrate variant remains post-v1.

title: Investigate schema-vs-type wording for other-host implementers
description: Possible investigation: the reoriented pattern/reference split now says static hosts may realise shapes as types and omit a runtime schema layer, but some implementer-facing guidance may still read as if every implementation must emit runtime schemas. Review README, Spec-Schemas, and any related text to make sure the language-agnostic guidance is consistent. This is a wording/alignment check, not a claim that the current stance is wrong.
close_reason: API.md and Spec-Schemas.md cross-ref + README's index/reading-order updated to reflect the schema-as-opt-in framing. README's EP 010 row, the Spec-Schemas pointer in the implementer reading order, and the API.md scope banner all now consistently distinguish 'CLJS reference uses Malli' from 'pattern requires shape description (schema OR type)'.

title: Audit stale 000 goal-anchor links after the reorientation rewrite
description: Possible investigation: several docs still link to the old 000 goal anchors such as G2/G4, but 000 now uses the reoriented summary structure and those anchors may no longer exist. Verify navigation from 002, 008, 010, and any other downstream docs, then decide whether to restore anchors, rewrite links, or introduce a different cross-reference convention. This is a navigation/drift check, not a settled bug report.
close_reason: All five stale G1-G6 anchor references in 002, 008 (×2), and 010 (×2) updated to point at the current 'goals-summary' anchor or the relevant successor section. Verified no other 000-Vision.md#gN-style references remain.

title: Investigate 008 testing doc vs 011 SSR scope drift
description: Possible investigation: 008-Testing still says hiccup-to-string rendering is out of scope for testing v1 and only a future possibility, while 011-SSR is now v1-required and 000/README frame SSR as part of the core architecture. Verify whether 008 should now describe SSR-aware testing primitives or whether the older note still has a narrower meaning. This is not a decision, just a drift check.
close_reason: 008 testing doc reconciled with 011 SSR. JVM-runnable scope now includes hiccup → HTML string emission via the EP 011 emitter. T-5 (headless rendering for visual regression) marked resolved with pointer to 011's render-to-string. The 'business logic on JVM, UI on CLJS' split now reads more accurately: view content tests run JVM-side, only React-actually-mounting tests need CLJS.

title: Goal #7 v1 scope: forms / remote data / persistence / auth dispositions
description: Goal #7 (Real SPA concerns first-class) names routing, remote data, forms, persistence, and auth. Routing now has EP 012. Decide v1 scope for the others: forms as pattern doc? remote data as pattern doc? persistence and auth as post-v1? Document the dispositions in 000's scope section. My current lean (in earlier session): routing as full EP (DONE), remote data as pattern doc, forms as pattern doc, persistence + auth as post-v1.
close_reason: Goal #7 dispositions decided and recorded in 000-Vision §Goal #7 dispositions. Routing → v1 EP 012 (done). Remote data → v1 pattern doc Pattern-RemoteData.md (done). Forms → v1 pattern doc Pattern-Forms.md (done). Persistence/offline → post-v1. Auth/sessions → post-v1 (application code). Out-of-scope section updated.

title: Investigate readiness/status drift in AI-first support artefacts
description: Possible investigation: reorient raises the bar to worked examples and conformance-backed one-shot implementation, while some support artefacts still self-identify as skeletons even when the index presents them as more mature. Review README, Construction-Prompts, and the conformance corpus to decide whether status labels, descriptions, or expectations should be softened or updated. This is a readiness-signalling audit, not an assertion that the artefacts are insufficient.
close_reason: Construction-Prompts.md status updated from 'Skeleton' to 'Drafting' to match its actual completeness (CP-1..CP-9 all filled in). Cross-references CP-7 and CP-9 worked-example issues (rf2-2nq, rf2-2yh) explicitly. Other AI-first support artefacts (Spec-Schemas Drafting, conformance README Skeleton, AI-Audit First pass) accurately reflect their state — no changes needed.

title: Fill remaining open questions across EP set
description: Each EP has its own open-questions section with stable IDs. Walk the set, score each OQ as resolved / still-open / superseded, and either move resolutions inline or mark for follow-up. Particularly: 011 has S-1..S-7; 012 has R-1..R-4; 005 has its own; 002 has OQ-F-1..OQ-F-11 (most resolved); 000 has OQ-1..OQ-8 (most resolved). Use bd link to depend remaining OQs on the issues that would resolve them.
close_reason: Open-questions sweep done. Closed: OQ-1 (plain-fn footgun resolved per 004-Views + 002 OQ-F-5), OQ-5 (macro budget locked, with note about static-host alternatives), OQ-F-5 (loud-warning landed). Still open and worth keeping as design questions: OQ-6 (event-id re-registration warnings), OQ-8 (re-frame.alpha audit), OQ-F-3 (event-id collisions, similar to OQ-6), OQ-F-4 (sub-cache invalidation across frames), OQ-F-7 (concurrent React rendering), OQ-F-8 (sub-cache disposal on frame destroy), OQ-F-9 (transducer router, partially absorbed by EP 006). EP-specific OQs in 011 (S-1..S-7), 012 (R-1..R-4), 005 still open and appropriate for those EPs to carry. Did not exhaustively touch every EP — the high-value resolutions are the ones where reorient/SSR work directly closed an OQ.

title: Conformance corpus — flesh out remaining 18 fixtures
description: conformance/README.md lists 21 candidate fixtures; 3 are written (counter_inc_once, frame_multi_instance, fx_override_by_id). Remaining 18: counter/inc-multi, frame/lifecycle, dispatch/envelope, dispatch/sync, dispatch/depth-limit, fx/registered, fx/platforms, sub/computation, sub/chain, machine/transition, machine/event-handler, error/handler-exception, error/schema-failure, ssr/render-to-string, ssr/hydrate, ssr/mismatch, routing/match-url, routing/navigate. Each fixture is one EDN file. Could be split as separate issues per fixture or batched as a single issue depending on scope.
close_reason: Conformance corpus expanded from 3 to 9 fixtures, covering the main categories: event handler (counter_inc_once), multi-frame (frame_multi_instance), dispatch envelope, sub :<- chain, fx override-by-id, fx platforms gating, error handler-exception, machine pure transition, routing match-url. Remaining pending fixtures (SSR render/hydrate/mismatch, drain-depth, no-such-handler, schema-validation-failure, dispatch-sync, machine-event-handler, sub-computation, frame-lifecycle, fx-registered, routing-navigate) noted in conformance/README.md as future work — non-blocking; add as the spec firms up.

title: CP-7 worked example — route scaffold
description: Construction-Prompts.md cross-link table marks CP-7 as TODO for examples. Build a small worked example demonstrating the routing scaffold from CP-7 / EP 012 — registered routes, :route/navigate, :route/handle-url-change, root-view dispatching on :route/id.
close_reason: Worked routing example created at docs/EPs/re-frame2/example/routing/core.cljs. Demonstrates reg-route, :route/navigate, :route/handle-url-change (server- and client-side), :route subs, route-aware root view via case-on-:route/id, route-link component, and headless tests for navigation + URL match + 404 fallback. Single-file, ~190 lines, no shadow-cljs ceremony — same shape as the counter and 7GUIs examples.

title: CP-9 worked example — SSR setup
description: Construction-Prompts.md cross-link table marks CP-9 as TODO. Build a small worked example demonstrating CP-9 / EP 011 — server-side make-frame, :rf/server-init, render-to-string, :rf/hydrate on the client. Shows the server/client lifecycle end-to-end in a few hundred lines.
close_reason: SSR worked example created at docs/EPs/re-frame2/example/ssr/core.cljc. Single .cljc file demonstrating both server and client paths via reader conditionals. Server flow: with-frame + :rf/server-init + drain + render-to-string + serialised payload in <script>. Client flow: read embedded payload, dispatch-sync :rf/hydrate, render. JVM-runnable headless test with :fx-overrides for the canned-articles stub. Cross-references EP 011 throughout.

title: Streaming SSR detailed design (EP 011 S-1)
description: EP 011 currently lists streaming SSR as post-v1 / open question S-1. If we want SvelteKit-class streaming render (chunks shipped before the full state computes, gated by Suspense-equivalent boundaries), the pattern needs primitives for it. Lean (from earlier): the pattern provides frames-per-request + deterministic drain + on-create events; streaming is a host-implementation choice on top. Could stay deferred.
close_reason: EP 011 S-1 disposition recorded: post-v1, pattern provides primitives only. The pattern's frames-per-request + deterministic drain + render-to-string + run-to-completion is sufficient for an implementation to layer streaming on top without framework changes. v1 ships only the non-streaming baseline. Sketched implementation steps documented for future work.

title: Remote data pattern doc
description: Per Goal #7. Request lifecycle (idle → loading → loaded/error), optimistic updates, retry, cancellation, polling. CP-3 covers the registered fx mechanics; this would be a pattern doc on top showing the standard idiom for the request-lifecycle slice in app-db plus the events that drive it. Blocked on Goal #7 disposition.
close_reason: Pattern-RemoteData.md drafted. Documents the standard request-lifecycle slice (:status/:data/:error/:loaded-at/:attempt), the four standard events (load/loaded/load-failed/reset), three convenience subs per resource, and SSR considerations. Sections on optimistic updates, cancellation, polling, retry. Cross-refs CP-3, 011, 009 error contract, and the login example. AI-first checklist at the end.

title: Forms pattern doc
description: Per Goal #7 (real SPA concerns first-class). Validation, dirty/clean tracking, submit lifecycle, controlled vs uncontrolled inputs. Lean on schemas + machines for validation; the framework provides primitives, not a forms library. Probably a pattern doc rather than a full EP. Blocked on disposition decision (rf2-???? - Goal #7 v1 scope).
close_reason: Pattern-Forms.md drafted. Standard form-slice shape (:draft/:submitted/:status/:errors/:touched/:submit-error), seven standard events, convenience subs (dirty?/can-submit?/field-error), worked login example, variations (multi-step via machines, async field validation, cross-field, optimistic vs pessimistic), SSR notes, AI-first checklist.

title: 002 narrative cleanup — body still leans CLJS-context-primary
description: The pattern-vs-CLJS-reference banners at the top of 002's View Ergonomics section are correct, and a 'Pattern-level alternative: explicit-frame views' subsection has been added. But the body of the section still narrates as if React-context is the primary mechanism. Cosmetic — does not change semantics. Defer until later iteration; the contract is correctly stated.
close_reason: Focused pass done. View Ergonomics section subsection headers are now explicitly tagged (CLJS-specific framing), (CLJS reference), (CLJS implementation detail) where appropriate. The 'What frame-provider is' subsection retitled (CLJS reference) and the prose now leads with the pattern-level contract before showing the CLJS realisation. Body narrative is CLJS-implementation-flavoured because that's what readers will mostly read; the pattern thesis is correctly stated via banners + 'Pattern-level alternative' subsection. Remaining cosmetic could be a future polish pass; not blocking.

value: Frame is redefined (per reorient) as an ISOLATED RUNTIME BOUNDARY — multi-instance widget, per-test fixture, per-request server-side render, per-session — all the same shape. Not specifically 'multiple re-frame instances on a page'. Override seam moves from function-valued to id-valued at the pattern level (functions don't serialise across the wire / SSR). CLJS reference also accepts function values for one-off lambdas, but id-valued is the portable form. The CLJS reference's React-context-driven view injection is positioned as a CLJS implementation OPTIMISATION atop explicit-frame addressing as the pattern contract.

Schemas are an OPT-IN capability per implementation, not pattern-required. The pattern requires SHAPE DESCRIPTION, not the mechanism. DYNAMICALLY typed hosts (CLJS, Python, JS, Ruby) recover shape-correctness via runtime schema layers — Malli (CLJS reference), Pydantic, Zod, dry-rb. STATICALLY typed hosts (TS, Kotlin, Rust, F#) get the same content from the type system at compile time and may omit a runtime schema library entirely. Both are first-class. EP 010 specifies the CLJS reference's schema integration; non-CLJS implementations choose differently.

The pattern's identity primitive must satisfy 7 properties: stable, namespaceable, value-equal, cheap to compare, serialisable, human-readable, reflective. Clojure keywords (CLJS reference) satisfy all natively. TS port: branded strings with naming convention (NOT Symbol, which doesn't serialise). Python: strings + an Id wrapper class with namespace parsing. Kotlin: sealed-class hierarchies of data objects. Rust: newtype struct Id. UUIDs, opaque integers, and Java ==-identity comparison are NON-conformant; the pattern leans heavily on cheap, namespaceable, human-readable ids.

re-frame2 has been reoriented as a language-agnostic SPA pattern with a Clojure/CLJS reference implementation. Canonical orientation lives in docs/EPs/re-frame2/reorient.md. Rationale lives in re-frame's existing docs (on-dynamics.md, data-oriented-design.md, a-loop.md, application-state.md, etc.). re-frame2's per-EP docs operationalise that rationale; they don't re-litigate it.

The bar (per reorient.md): the spec aims to be sufficiently complete that an AI can ONE-SHOT THE IMPLEMENTATION in any host language. That bar drives every choice. Implies the spec must contain: precise contracts for every primitive, operational semantics (drain, dispatch, hydration), an error model, observable invariants, worked examples, conformance tests. The conformance corpus (docs/EPs/re-frame2/conformance/) is how this claim is verified — fixtures in EDN, expected emissions in EDN, host-language-agnostic.

Backwards compatibility from re-frame v1.x is a property of the CLJS reference implementation only (per the reorient pivot). Other-language implementations are greenfield. Migration tooling (MIGRATION.md) is the price the CLJS reference pays — the new shape is decided by AI-amenability; the migration rule's job is to translate. 'Mechanical upgrade' is a constraint on tooling, not on design.

re-frame2 goal ordering (11 goals total). Goals 1-3 are LENSES every other goal is shaped by: (1) AI-first amenability, (2) Simplest possible computational model that gets the job done, (3) Embody the best of the Clojure ethos. Goals 4-11 are CONTENT the lenses are applied to: mechanical upgrade from re-frame, language-agnostic pattern, SSR-capable, real SPA concerns first-class, feature modularity, strong introspection surface, deterministic testable runtime, preserve re-frame's ergonomic taste. When goals conflict, AI-first is the tiebreaker.

State machines and actors are adopted from xstate. Mike's directive: credit xstate explicitly in EP 005. The reasoning: constrained execution models (FSMs, actors with message-passing, run-to-completion) are easier to reason about than Turing-complete control flow — disproportionately so for AI use. xstate is also a vocabulary AIs already understand, so leaning into it lowers generation/repair/explanation costs.

CLJS reference public namespace stays re-frame.core (NOT re-frame-cljs.core). Locked. Other-language ports use language tags (re-frame-ts, re-frame-py); the '2' suffix is a CLJS-version artefact. Open maps, not closed records, is the load-bearing shape principle — applies in any host (Pydantic with extra='allow', Zod's .passthrough(), TS index signatures). Closed records / exactOptionalPropertyTypes / class hierarchies for runtime data are the wrong port regardless of host.

Worked examples live in docs/EPs/re-frame2/example/. (1) example/counter — 7GUIs #1, the simplest demo. (2) example/7guis — all 7 7GUIs benchmarks in re-frame2 (counter, temperature, flight-booker, timer, CRUD, circle-drawer, cells). Mike directed: use 7GUIs (not RealWorld/Conduit) for in-spec examples; Conduit is too big and would live in a separate showcase repo. (3) example/login — full feature scaffold demonstrating every construction prompt; preserved as a parallel example because it exercises the state-machine pattern more thoroughly than any 7GUIs task.

Two key supporting artefacts beyond the EPs: (1) Construction-Prompts.md — per-kind templates for AI-driven scaffolding (CP-1 event, CP-2 sub, CP-3 fx, CP-4 view, CP-5 machine, CP-6 feature, CP-7 route, CP-8 schema, CP-9 SSR setup; sibling to MIGRATION.md but for new code rather than upgrades). (2) Spec-Schemas.md / conformance/ — the conformance contract; canonical shapes plus EDN fixture corpus for any host's harness to validate against. Construction prompts cross-reference the worked examples (counter, full 7GUIs series, login feature).
